FIGURE 63. The scattering of light involves a photon going into an electron and a photon coming out—not necessarily in that order, as seen in example (b). The example in (c) shows a strange but real possibility: the electron emits a photon, rushes backwards in time to absorb a photon, and then continues forwards in time.

当时间向前推移时，向后运动的电子看起来与普通电子相同，只是它会被普通电子吸引——我们称它带“正电荷”。（如果我考虑极化效应，就能清楚地解释为什么向后运动电子的j符号会反转，从而使其电荷看起来是正的。）因此，它被称为“正电子”。正电子是电子的姊妹粒子，也是“反粒子”的一个例子。 8

The backwards-moving electron when viewed with time moving forwards appears the same as an ordinary electron, except it’s attracted to normal electrons—we say it has a “positive charge.” (Had I included the effects of polarization, it would be apparent why the sign of j for the backwards-moving electron appears reversed, making the charge appear positive.) For this reason it’s called a “positron.” The positron is a sister particle to the electron, and is an example of an “anti-particle.”8

这种现象是普遍存在的。自然界中的每个粒子都具有逆时间运动的振幅，因此都存在反粒子。当一个粒子与其反粒子碰撞时，它们会相互湮灭并形成其他粒子。（对于正电子和电子的湮灭，通常会产生一个或两个光子。）那么光子呢？正如我们之前看到的，光子在逆时间运动时，其所有特征都与自身完全相同，因此它们是自身的反粒子。瞧，我们多么巧妙地将例外融入了规则之中！

This phenomenon is general. Every particle in Nature has an amplitude to move backwards in time, and therefore has an anti-particle. When a particle and its anti-particle collide, they annihilate each other and form other particles. (For positrons and electrons annihilating, it is usually a photon or two.) And what about photons? Photons look exactly the same in all respects when they travel backwards in time—as we saw earlier—so they are their own anti-particles. You see how clever we are at making an exception part of the rule!

我想向你们展示一下，随着时间推移，这个向后运动的电子在我们眼中是什么样子。为了便于观察，我用一系列平行线将图表划分成若干时间段，从 T0到T10 （见图64 ）。我们从T0 时刻开始，一个电子朝着一个向相反方向运动的光子运动。突然——在 T3 时刻——光子分裂成两个粒子，一个正电子和一个负电子。电子。正电子的寿命很短：它很快就会与电子相遇——在 T5 时刻，它们湮灭并产生一个新的光子。与此同时，之前由原始光子产生的电子则继续在时空中传播。

I’d like to show you what this backwards-moving electron looks like to us, as we move forwards in time. With a sequence of parallel lines to aid the eye, I’m going to divide the diagram into blocks of time, T0 to T10 (see Fig. 64). We start at T0 with an electron moving toward a photon, which is moving in the opposite direction. All of a sudden—at T3—the photon turns into two particles, a positron and an electron. The positron doesn’t last very long: it soon runs into the electron—at T5, where they annihilate and produce a new photon. Meanwhile, the electron created earlier by the original photon continues on through space-time.

图64。观察图 63 中的示例 (c)，仅沿时间向前推进（正如我们在实验室中被迫做的那样），从 T0到T3 ，我们看到电子和光子相互靠近。突然，在 T3 时刻，光子“衰变”，出现两个粒子——一个电子和一种新型粒子（称为“正电子”），这个正电子实际上是一个沿时间向后运动的电子，并且看起来正朝着原来的电子（它自己！）运动。在 T5 时刻，正电子与原来的电子湮灭，产生一个新的光子。与此同时，由先前光子产生的电子继续在时空中向前运动。这一系列事件已在实验室中被观察到，并且无需任何修改即可自动包含在 E(A 到 B) 的公式中。

FIGURE 64. Looking at example (c) from Fig. 63 going only forwards in time (as we are forced to do in the laboratory), from T0 to T3 we see the electron and photon moving toward each other. All of a sudden, at T3 the photon “disintegrates” and two particles appear—an electron and a new kind of particle (called a “positron”) which is an electron going backwards in time and which appears to move toward the original electron (itself!). At T5 the positron annihilates with the original electron to produce a new photon. Meanwhile, the electron created by the earlier photon continues forwards in space-time. This sequence of events has been observed in the laboratory, and is included automatically in the formula for E(A to B) without any modification.

接下来我想谈谈原子中的电子。为了理解原子中电子的行为，我们需要引入另一个要素：原子核——位于原子中心的较重部分，它至少包含一个质子（质子就像一个“潘多拉魔盒”，我们将在下一讲中打开）。这讲我不会详细讲解原子核行为的规律，因为它们非常复杂。但在这个原子核处于静止状态的情况下，我们可以将其行为近似为一个粒子，它具有一定的振幅，可以根据公式 E(A 到 B) 在时空中从一个位置运动到另一个位置，但n 的值要大得多。由于原子核相对于电子来说非常重，我们可以近似地认为它在时间中基本保持静止。

The next thing I would like to talk about is an electron in an atom. In order to understand the behavior of electrons in atoms, we have to add one other feature, the nucleus—the heavy part at the center of an atom that contains at least one proton (a proton is a “Pandora’s Box” that we will open in the next lecture). I will not give you the correct laws for the behavior of the nucleus in this lecture; they are very complicated. But in this case, where the nucleus is quiet, we can approximate its behavior as that of a particle with an amplitude to go from one place to another in space-time according to the formula for E(A to B), but with a much higher number for n. Since the nucleus is so heavy compared to an electron, we can deal with it approximately here by saying that it stays in essentially one place as it moves through time.

最简单的原子叫做氢原子，它由一个质子和一个电子组成。通过交换光子，质子使电子保持在附近，围绕着它运动（见图65）。9含有多个质子和相应数量电子的原子也会散射光（空气中的原子散射太阳光，使天空呈现蓝色），但是这些原子的结构图会包含太多直线和曲线，简直一团糟！

The simplest atom, called hydrogen, is a proton and an electron. By exchanging photons, the proton keeps the electron nearby, dancing around it (see Fig. 65).9 Atoms that contain more than one proton and the corresponding number of electrons also scatter light (atoms in the air scatter light from the sun and make the sky blue), but the diagrams for these atoms would involve so many straight and wiggly lines that they’d be a complete mess!

图65.电子通过与质子进行光子交换，被限制在原子核附近的一定距离范围内（我们将在第四章探讨这个“潘多拉魔盒”）。目前，我们可以将质子近似视为静止粒子。图中所示为氢原子，它由一个质子和一个电子组成，二者之间正在进行光子交换。

FIGURE 65. An electron is kept within a certain range of distance to the nucleus of an atom by photon exchanges with a proton (a “Pandora’s Box” that we will look into in Chapter 4). For now, the proton can be approximated as a stationary particle. Shown here is a hydrogen atom, consisting of a proton and an electron exchanging photons.

图66.原子中电子对光的散射现象是造成玻璃层部分反射的原因。该图展示了氢原子中发生这种现象的一种方式。

FIGURE 66. The scattering of light by an electron in an atom is the phenomenon that accounts for partial reflection in a layer of glass. The diagram shows one way this event can happen in a hydrogen atom.

现在，我想给你们展示一下氢原子中电子散射光的示意图（见图66）。当电子和原子核交换光子时，一个光子会进入原子核。来自原子外部的光子撞击电子并被吸收；然后，一个新的光子被发射出来。（当然，还有其他可能性需要考虑，例如，新的光子可能在旧光子被吸收之前就被发射出来。）电子散射光子的所有方式的总振幅可以概括为一个箭头，表示一定程度的收缩和旋转。（稍后，我们将这个箭头称为“S”。）这个程度取决于原子核和原子中电子的排列方式，并且对于不同的材料也不同。

Now, I’d like to show you a diagram of an electron in a hydrogen atom scattering light (see Fig. 66). As the electron and the nucleus are exchanging photons, a photon comes from outside the atom, hits the electron and is absorbed; then a new photon is emitted. (As usual, there are other possibilities to be considered, such as the new photon is emitted before the old photon is absorbed.) The total amplitude for all the ways an electron can scatter a photon can be summed up as a single arrow, a certain amount of shrink and turn. (Later, we will call this arrow “S.”) This amount depends on the nucleus and the arrangement of the electrons in the atoms, and is different for different materials.

现在，我们再来看看光在玻璃层上的部分反射。它是如何运作的呢？我之前提到过光会从玻璃的前表面和后表面反射。我一开始为了简化问题，就用了“表面”这个概念。实际上，光并不受表面的影响。入射光子会被玻璃内部原子中的电子散射，然后一个新的光子会反射回探测器。有趣的是，我们不用把玻璃内部所有电子散射入射光子的数十亿个小箭头加起来，只需要把“前表面”和“后表面”的反射加起来，就能得到相同的结果。让我们来看看为什么。

Now, let’s look again at the partial reflection of light by a layer of glass. How does it work? I talked about light being reflected from the front surface and the back surface. This idea of surfaces was a simplification I made in order to keep things easy at the beginning. Light is really not affected by surfaces. An incoming photon is scattered by the electrons in the atoms inside the glass, and a new photon comes back up to the detector. It’s interesting that instead of adding up all the billions of tiny arrows that represent the amplitude for all the electrons inside the glass to scatter an incoming photon, we can add just two arrows—for the “front surface” and “back surface” reflections—and come out with the same answer. Let’s see why.

要从新的角度讨论层反射，我们必须考虑时间维度。之前，当我们讨论单色光源发出的光时，我们用一个假想的秒表来计时光子的运动——秒表的指针决定了给定路径的振幅角度。在 P(A 到 B)（光子从一点到另一点的振幅）的公式中，没有提到任何转向。秒表去哪儿了？转向又去哪儿了？

To discuss reflection by a layer from our new point of view we must take into account the dimension of time. Previously, when we talked about light from a monochromatic source, we used an imaginary stopwatch that times a photon as it moves—the hand of this stopwatch determined the angle of the amplitude for a given path. In the formula for P(A to B) (the amplitude for a photon to go from point to point) there is no mention of any turning. What happened to the stopwatch? What happened to the turning?

在第一讲中，我只是简单地提到光源是单色光。为了正确分析薄层的局部反射，我们需要了解更多关于单色光的知识。光源。光源发射光子的振幅通常随时间变化：随着时间的推移，光源发射光子的振幅角度会发生变化。白光光源（多种颜色混合在一起）以混沌的方式发射光子：振幅角度会突然且不规则地断断续续地变化。但是，当我们构建单色光源时，我们制造的装置经过精心设计，使得在特定时刻发射光子的振幅很容易计算：它的角度变化速度恒定，就像秒表指针一样。（实际上，这个箭头的旋转速度与我们之前使用的假想秒表相同，但方向相反——参见图 67。）

In the first lecture I simply said that the light source was monochromatic. To correctly analyze partial reflection by a layer, we need to know more about a monochromatic light source. The amplitude for a photon to be emitted by a source varies, in general, with the time: as time goes on, the angle of the amplitude for a photon to be emitted by a source changes. A source of white light—many colors mixed together—emits photons in a chaotic manner: the angle of the amplitude changes abruptly and irregularly in fits and starts. But when we construct a monochromatic source, we are making a device that has been carefully arranged so that the amplitude for a photon to be emitted at a certain time is easily calculated: it changes its angle at a constant speed, like a stopwatch hand. (Actually, this arrow turns at the same speed as the imaginary stopwatch we used before, but in the opposite direction—see Fig. 67.)

图67.单色光源是一种结构精巧的装置，它以非常可预测的方式发射光子：在某一时刻发射光子的振幅随时间推移逆时针旋转。因此，光源在稍后时刻发射光子的振幅角度较小。假设光源发出的所有光都以速度c传播（因为距离很远）。

FIGURE 67. A monochromatic source is a beautifully constructed apparatus that emits a photon in a very predictable way: the amplitude for a photon to be emitted at a certain time rotates counterclockwise as time moves forwards. Thus the amplitude for the source to emit a photon at a later time has a lesser angle. It will be assumed that all the light emitted from the source goes at speed c (since the distances are large).

旋转速率取决于光的颜色：蓝色光源的振幅旋转速度几乎是红色光源的两倍，这一点与之前一样。因此，我们用于“虚拟秒表”的计时器是单色光源：——实际上，给定波长下振幅的角度路径取决于光子从光源发射的时间。

The rate of turning depends on the color of the light: the amplitude for a blue source turns nearly twice as fast as that for a red source, just as before. So the timer we used for the “imaginary stopwatch” was the monochromatic source:—in reality, the angle of the amplitude for a given path depends on what time the photon is emitted from the source.

一旦光子被发射，当光子在时空中从一点传播到另一点时，箭头方向不再改变。虽然公式 P(A 到 B) 表明光子可以以非 c 的速度从一点传播到另一点，但在我们的实验中，光源到探测器的距离相对较大（与原子相比），因此 P(A 到 B) 的长度中唯一起作用的贡献来自速度c。

Once a photon has been emitted, there is no further turning of the arrow as a photon goes from one point to another in space-time. Although the formula P(A to B) says that there is an amplitude for light to go from one place to another at speeds other than c, the distance from the source to the detector in our experiment is relatively large (compared to an atom), so the only surviving contribution to P(A to B)’s length that counts comes from speed c.

为了开始新的部分反射计算，我们首先要完整定义事件：A 点的探测器在某一时刻T发出咔嗒声。然后，我们将玻璃层分割成若干个极薄的扇形区域——比如说六个（见图68a）。根据我们在第二讲中的分析，我们发现几乎所有的光都从镜子的中心反射，我们知道，尽管每个电子都会向各个方向散射光，但当把每个扇形区域的所有箭头相加时，唯一不会相互抵消的地方是光线垂直向下到达扇形区域中心并向两个方向之一散射的地方——向上反射回到探测器或向下穿过玻璃。因此，事件的最终箭头将由六个箭头相加得出，这六个箭头分别代表从玻璃层中垂直排列的六个中心点（X1到X6 ）散射的光。

To begin our new calculation of partial reflection, let’s start by defining the event completely: the detector at A makes a click at a certain time, T. Then, let’s divide the layer of glass into a number of very thin sections—let’s say, six (see Fig. 68a). From the analysis we did in the second lecture in which we found that nearly all the light is reflected from the middle of a mirror, we know that although each electron is scattering light in all directions, when all the arrows for each section are added, the only place where they don’t cancel out is where light goes straight down to the middle of the section and scatters in one of two directions—straight back up to the detector or straight down through the glass. The final arrow for the event will thus be determined by adding the six arrows representing the scattering of light from the six middle points—X1 to X6—arranged vertically throughout the glass.

好的，我们来计算光线可能经过的六个点 X1 到 X6 的每种路径的箭头。每种路径都涉及四个步骤（这意味着需要计算四个箭头）：

All right, let’s calculate the arrow for each of these ways the light could go—via the six points, X1 to X6. There are four steps involved in each way (which means four arrows will be multiplied):

—步骤1：光源在某一时刻发射一个光子。

—STEP #1: A photon is emitted by the source at a certain time.

—步骤2：光子从光源到达玻璃中的某一点。

—STEP #2: The photon goes from the source to one of the points in the glass.

—步骤3：此时光子被电子散射。

—STEP #3: The photon is scattered by an electron at that point.

—步骤4：一个新的光子到达探测器。

—STEP #4: A new photon makes its way up to the detector.

图68。我们首先将一层玻璃分成若干部分（此处为六部分），然后考察光线从光源到玻璃再返回到 A 点探测器的各种路径，以此开始对部分反射进行新的分析。玻璃中唯一重要的点（散射光振幅不会相互抵消的点）位于每个部分的中间；图 (a) 显示了 X1到X6在玻璃内部的实际位置，图 (b) 则显示了它们在时空图上的垂直线。我们计算概率的事件是：A 点探测器在某个时刻 T 发出咔嗒声。因此，该事件在时空图上表现为一个点（A 和 T 的交​​点）。

FIGURE 68. We begin our new analysis of partial reflection by dividing a layer of glass into a number of sections (here, six), and looking at the various ways the light could go from the source to the glass and back up to the detector at A. The only important points in the glass (where the amplitudes for scattering light don’t cancel out) are located at the middle of each section; X1 to X6 are shown in (a) at their physical location inside the glass, and in (b) as vertical lines on the space-time graph. The event whose probability we are calculating is: the detector at A makes a click at a certain time, T. Thus the event appears as a point (where A and T intersect) on the space-time graph.

对于事件发生的每一种方式，都需要连续进行四个步骤，因此需要四个箭头相乘。这些步骤如图 (b) 所示：1) 光子在某一时刻离开光源（箭头 T1至T6分别代表步骤 1 的振幅不同：1) 光子从光源到达玻璃中的某个点（六个不同位置用向右上方的波浪线表示）；2) 其中一个点的电子散射一个光子（用短而宽的垂直线表示）；3) 一个新的光子到达探测器，并在指定时间 T 到达（用向左上方的波浪线表示）。步骤 2、3 和 4 的振幅对于六个不同位置都相同，而步骤 1 的振幅则不同：与玻璃顶部（X1 处）的电子散射的光子相比，在玻璃深处（例如 X2 处）散射的光子必须更早离开光源，即在T2时刻。

For each of the ways the event can happen, four steps must occur in succession, so four arrows have to be multiplied. The steps are shown in (b): 1) a photon leaves the source at a certain time (the arrows at T1 to T6 represent the amplitude to do that for six different times); 2) the photon goes from the source to one of the points in the glass (the six alternatives are depicted as wavy lines going up to the right); 3) an electron at one of the points scatters a photon (shown as short, wide vertical lines); and 4) a new photon goes to the detector and arrives at the appointed time, T (shown as a wavy line going up to the left). The amplitudes for steps 2, 3, and 4 are the same for the six alternatives, while the amplitudes for step 1 are different: compared to a photon scattered by an electron at the top of the glass (at X1), a photon scattered deeper in the glass—at X2, for example—must leave the source earlier, at T2.

当我们完成每种方案的四个箭头相乘后，得到的箭头如图 (c) 所示，比图 (b) 中的箭头短；每个箭头都旋转了 90°（符合玻璃中电子的散射特性）。将这六个箭头按顺序相加，它们形成一个弧；最后一个箭头是该弧的弦。同样的最后一个箭头也可以通过绘制两个半径箭头（如图 (d) 所示）并将它们“相减”（将“前表面”箭头反向旋转并将其加到“后表面”箭头上）来获得。这种简便方法在第一讲中被用作简化步骤。

When we are finished multiplying the four arrows for each alternative, the resulting arrows, shown in (c), are shorter than those in (b); each has been turned 90° (in accordance with the scattering characteristics of electrons in glass). When these six arrows are added together in order, they form an arc; the final arrow is its chord. The same final arrow can be obtained by drawing two radius arrows, shown in (d), and “subtracting” them (turning the “front surface” arrow around in the opposite direction and adding it to the “back surface” arrow). This shortcut was used as a simplification in the first lecture.

我们将步骤 2 和 4（光子在玻璃中往返于某一点）的振幅视为不涉及收缩或旋转，因为我们可以假设光源与玻璃之间或玻璃与探测器之间没有光损耗或扩散。对于步骤 3（电子散射光子），散射振幅是一个常数——收缩并旋转一定角度 S——并且在玻璃中处处相同。（如前所述，该角度 S 因材料而异。对于玻璃，旋转角度 S 为 90°。）因此，在需要相乘的四个箭头中，只有步骤 1 的箭头——即在某一时刻从光源发射的光子的振幅——在不同的计算方案中有所不同。

We will say the amplitudes for steps 2 and 4 (a photon goes to or from a point in the glass) involve no shrinking or turning, because we can assume that none of the light gets lost or spread out between the source and the glass or between the glass and the detector. For step 3 (an electron scatters a photon) the amplitude for scattering is a constant—a shrink and a turn by a certain amount, S—and is the same everywhere in the glass. (This amount is, as I mentioned before, different for different materials. For glass, the turn of S is 90°.) Therefore, of the four arrows to be multiplied, only the arrow for step 1—the amplitude for a photon to be emitted from the source at a certain time—is different from one alternative to the next.

光子必须在时间 T 被发射才能到达探测器 A（见图68b）六条不同的路径的情况并不相同。被 X₂ 散射的光子必须比被X₁散射的光子稍早发射，因为 X₂ 路径更长。因此，T₂ 处的箭头比T₁处的箭头旋转的角度略大，因为单色光源在特定时刻发射光子的振幅会随着时间的推移而逆时针旋转。同样的情况也适用于 T₆ 之前的每个箭头：所有六个箭头的长度相同，但它们的旋转角度不同——也就是说，它们指向不同的方向——因为它们代表光源在不同时刻发射的光子。

The time at which a photon would have to have been emitted to reach the detector A at time T (see Fig. 68b) is not the same for the six different paths. A photon scatterd by X2 would have to have been emitted slightly earlier than a photon scattered by X1 because that path is longer. Thus the arrow at T2 is turned slightly more than the arrow at T1 because the amplitude for a monochromatic source to emit a photon at a certain time rotates counterclockwise as time goes on. The same goes for each arrow down to T6: all six arrows have the same length, but they are turned at different angles—that is, they are pointing in different directions—because they represent a photon emitted by the source at different times.

将T1处的箭头按照步骤 2、3 和 4 中规定的量缩短，并按照步骤 3 中规定的量旋转 90° 后，即可得到箭头 1（见图68c ）。箭头 2 至 6 的处理方法相同。因此，箭头 1 至 6 的长度（缩短后的长度）相同，并且它们彼此之间的旋转角度与T1至 T6处的箭头旋转角度完全相同。

After shrinking the arrow at T1 by the amounts prescribed in steps 2, 3 and 4—and turning it the 90° prescribed in step 3—we end up with arrow 1 (see Fig. 68c). The same goes for the arrows 2 through 6. Thus arrows 1 through 6 are all the same (shortened) length, and are turned relative to each other in exactly the same amount as the arrows at T1 through T6.

接下来，我们添加箭头 1 到 6。按顺序连接这些箭头（从 1 到 6），我们得到类似弧线或圆的一部分。最后一个箭头构成这条弧线的弦。最后一个箭头的长度随着玻璃厚度的增加而增加——玻璃越厚，分割的部分就越多，箭头就越多，因此圆的弧度也就越大——直到达到半个圆（此时最后一个箭头就是圆的直径）。然后，随着玻璃厚度的继续增加，最后一个箭头的长度逐渐减小，圆最终形成一个完整的圆，开始一个新的循环。这个长度的平方就是事件发生的概率，其值在 0% 到 16% 之间循环变化。

Next, we add arrows 1 to 6. Connecting the arrows in order from 1 to 6, we get something like an arc, or part of a circle. The final arrow forms the chord of this arc. The length of the final arrow increases with the thickness of the glass—thicker glass means more sections, more arrows, and therefore more of a circle—until half a circle is reached (and the final arrow is its diameter). Then the length of the final arrow decreases as the thickness of the glass continues to increase, and the circle becomes complete to begin a new cycle. The square of this length is the probability of the event, and it varies in the cycle of zero to 16%.

我们可以用一个数学技巧来得到相同的答案（见图68d）：如果我们从“圆”的中心分别向箭头 1 的尾部和箭头 6 的头部画箭头，就会得到两个半径。如果将从中心到箭头 1 的半径箭头旋转 180°（“减去”），那么它就可以与另一个半径箭头合并，从而得到……同样的最终箭头！这就是我在第一讲中所做的：这两个半径就是我所说的代表“前表面”和“后表面”反射的两个箭头。它们的长度都是著名的0.2。10

There is a mathematical trick we can use to get the same answer (see Fig. 68d): If we draw arrows from the center of the “circle” to the tail of arrow 1 and to the head of arrow 6, we get two radii. If the radius arrow from the center to arrow 1 is turned 180° (“subtracted”), then it can be combined with the other radius arrow to give us the same final arrow! That’s what I was doing in the first lecture: these two radii are the two arrows I said represented the “front surface” and “back surface” reflections. They each have the famous length of 0.2.10

因此，我们可以通过（错误地）假设所有反射都来自玻璃的前后表面来得到部分反射概率的正确答案。在这个直观简单的分析中，“前表面”和“后表面”箭头是数学构造，可以得出正确答案；而我们刚才进行的分析——用时空图和构成圆一部分的箭头——则更准确地描述了实际情况：部分反射是玻璃内部电子对光的散射。

Thus we can get the correct answer for the probability of partial reflection by imagining (falsely) that all reflection comes from only the front and back surfaces. In this intuitively easy analysis, the “front surface” and “back surface” arrows are mathematical constructions that give us the right answer, whereas the analysis we just did—with the space-time drawing and the arrows forming part of a circle—is a more accurate representation of what is really going on: partial reflection is the scattering of light by electrons inside the glass.

那么，穿过玻璃层的光又如何呢？首先，存在一种振幅，光子可以不与任何电子碰撞就直接穿过玻璃（见图69a）。就长度而言，这是最重要的箭头。但光子到达玻璃下方探测器还有其他六种途径：光子可以撞击 X1并将新光子散射到 B；光子可以撞击 X2并将新光子散射到 B，以此类推。这六个箭头的长度与前一个例子中构成“圆”的箭头长度相同：它们的长度基于玻璃中电子散射光子的相同振幅 S。但这一次，所有六个箭头都指向同一方向，因为涉及一次散射的六条路径的长度相同。对于玻璃等透明物质，这些次要箭头的方向与主箭头垂直。当多个辅助箭头添加到主箭头上时，最终会形成一个与主箭头长度相同但方向略有不同的箭头。玻璃越厚，辅助箭头就越多，最终箭头的旋转角度也就越大。这就是聚焦透镜的工作原理：通过在较短的光路中插入额外的玻璃层，可以使所有光路的最终箭头指向同一方向。

Now, what about the light that goes through the layer of glass? First, there is an amplitude that the photon goes straight through the glass without hitting any electrons (see Fig. 69a). This is the most important arrow in terms of length. But there are six other ways a photon could reach the detector below the glass: a photon could hit X1 and scatter the new photon down to B; a photon could hit X2 and scatter the new photon down to B, and so on. These six arrows all have the same length as the arrows that formed the “circle” in the previous example: their length is based on that same amplitude of an electron in the glass to scatter a photon, S. But this time, all six arrows point in the same direction, because the length of all six paths that involve one scattering is the same. The direction of these minor arrows is at right angles to the main arrow for transparent substances such as glass. When the minor arrows are added to the main arrow, they result in a final arrow that has the same length as the main arrow, but is turned in a slightly different direction. The thicker the glass, the more minor arrows there are, and the more the final arrow is turned. That’s how a focusing lens really works: the final arrows for all the paths can be made to point in the same direction by inserting extra thicknesses of glass into the shorter paths.

图69。穿过玻璃层到达探测器 B点的光的最大振幅来自玻璃内部电子未发生散射的部分，如图 (a) 所示。我们在此箭头上添加六个小箭头，分别代表光从玻璃内部各部分（由点 X1到X6表示）的散射。这六个箭头长度相同（因为玻璃中任何位置的散射振幅都相同），方向也相同（因为从光源经任意点 X 到 B 的路径长度都相同）。将这些小箭头添加到大箭头后，我们发现光穿过玻璃层的最终箭头的偏转角度比我们预期的要大，而我们预期光线是直接入射的。因此，我们认为光穿过玻璃所需的时间比穿过真空或空气所需的时间更长。材料中电子引起的最终箭头偏转角度称为“折射率”。

FIGURE 69. The largest amplitude for light that is transmitted through the layer of glass to the detector at B comes from the part that represents no scattering by the electrons inside the glass, shown in (a). To this arrow we add six small arrows that represent the scattering of light from each of the sections, represented by points X1 to X6. These six arrows have the same length (because the amplitude for scattering is the same anywhere in the glass) and point in the same direction (because the length of each path from the source through any point X to B is the same). After adding the small arrows to the large one, we find the final arrow for the transmission of light through a layer of glass is turned more than what we would have expected if the light came only directly. For this reason it appears to us that light takes longer to go through glass than it takes to go through a vacuum or through air. The amount of turning by the final arrow caused by the electrons in a material is called the “index of refraction.”

对于透明材料，小箭​​头与主箭头垂直（实际上，当考虑双重和三重散射时，小箭头会弯曲，从而保证最终箭头的长度不超过主箭头：自然界总是会进行精确计算，确保我们接收到的光量永远不会超过输入的光量）。对于部分不透明（即部分吸收光）的材料，小箭​​头指向主箭头，导致最终箭头明显短于预期，如图 (b) 所示。这种较短的最终箭头表示光子穿过部分不透明材料的概率降低。

For transparent materials, the little arrows are at right angles to the main arrow (they actually curve around when we include double and triple scatterings, keeping the final arrow from being longer than the main arrow: Nature always has it worked out so we never get more light out than we put in). For materials that are partially opaque—that absorb light to an extent—the little arrows point toward the main arrow, resulting in a final arrow that is significantly shorter than expected, shown in (b). This shorter final arrow represents a reduced probability of a photon being transmitted through partially opaque material.

如果光子在玻璃中传播的速度比在空气中慢，也会出现同样的效果：最终的箭头会发生额外的偏转。这就是为什么我之前说光在玻璃（或水）中传播的速度看起来比在空气中慢。实际上，光速的“减慢”是由于玻璃（或水）中的原子散射光线而导致的额外偏转。光线穿过某种材料时最终箭头的额外偏转程度称为该材料的“折射率”。 11

The same effect would appear if photons went slower through glass than through air: there would be extra turning of the final arrow. That’s why I said earlier that light appears to go slower through glass (or water) than through air. In reality, the “slowing” of the light is extra turning caused by the atoms in the glass (or water) scattering the light. The degree to which there is extra turning of the final arrow as light goes through a given material is called its “index of refraction.”11

对于吸收光的物质，短箭头指向与主箭头的夹角小于直角（见图69b）。这导致末端箭头比主箭头短，表明光子穿过部分不透明玻璃的概率小于穿过透明玻璃的概率。

For substances that absorb light, the minor arrows are at less than right angles to the main arrow (see Fig. 69b). This causes the final arrow to be shorter than the main arrow, indicating that the probability of a photon going through partially opaque glass is smaller than through transparent glass.

因此，前两讲中提到的所有现象和任意数字——例如振幅为 0.2 的部分反射、光在水和玻璃中的“减速”等等——都可以通过这三个基本动作更详细地解释——这三个动作实际上也解释了几乎所有其他现象。

Thus it is that all the phenomena and the arbitrary numbers mentioned in the first two lectures—such as partial reflection with an amplitude of 0.2, the “slowing” of light in water and glass, and so on—are explained in more detail by just the three basic actions—three actions that do, in fact, explain nearly everything else, too.

很难相信，自然界中几乎所有看似巨大的多样性都源于这三种基本动作的重复组合。但事实的确如此。我将简要概述一下这种多样性是如何产生的。

It is hard to believe that nearly all the vast apparent variety in Nature results from the monotony of repeatedly combining just these three basic actions. But it does. I’ll outline a bit of how some of this variety arises.

我们可以从光子入手（见图 70）。时空中的 1 点和 2 点处的两个光子分别到达 3 点和 4 点的两个探测器的概率是多少？这一事件主要有两种发生方式，每种方式都取决于两个同时发生的因素：光子可以直接到达 3 点——P(1 到 3)*P(2 到 4)——或者它们可以“交叉”到达 4 点——P(1 到 4)*P(2 到 3)。将这两种可能性的振幅相加，由于存在干涉（正如我们在第二讲中看到的），最终的箭头长度会根据时空中各点的相对位置而变化。

We may start with photons (see Fig. 70). What is the probability that two photons, at points 1 and 2 in space-time, go to two detectors, at points 3 and 4? There are two main ways this event could happen and each depends on two things happening concomitantly: the photons could go directly—P(1 to 3)*P(2 to 4)—or they could “cross over”—P(1 to 4)*P(2 to 3). The resulting amplitudes for these two possibilities are added, and there is interference (as we saw in the second lecture), making the final arrow vary in length, depending on the relative location of the points in space-time.

如果我们把时空中的 3 和 4 点设为同一点（见图71）会怎样？假设两个光子最终都到达点 3，看看这会如何影响事件发生的概率。现在我们有 P(1 到 3)*P(2 到 3) 和 P(2 到 3)*P(1 到 3)，它们分别对应两个相同的箭头。将它们相加，其和是单个箭头长度的两倍，最终得到的箭头的平方是单个箭头平方的四倍。由于这两个箭头相同，它们总是“对齐”的。换句话说，干涉效应不会随着点 1 和点 2 之间的相对距离而波动；它始终为正。如果我们不考虑两个光子始终为正的干涉效应，我们应该认为平均概率会是原来的两倍。但实际上，我们始终得到四倍的概率。当涉及多个光子时，这种超出预期的概率还会进一步增加。

What if we make 3 and 4 the same point in space-time (see Fig. 71)? Let’s say both photons end up at point 3, and see how this affects the probability of the event. Now we have P(1 to 3)*P(2 to 3) and P(2 to 3)*P(1 to 3), which result in two identical arrows. When added, their sum is twice the length of either one, and produces a final arrow whose square is four times the square of either arrow alone. Be-cause the two arrows are identical, they are always “lined up.” In other words, the interference doesn’t fluctuate according to the relative separation between points 1 and 2; it is always positive. If we didn’t think about the always positive interference of the two photons, we should have thought that we would get twice the probability, on average. Instead, we get four times the probability all the time. When many photons are involved, this more-than-expected probability increases even further.

图70.时空中的 1 点和 2 点处的光子到达 3 点和 4 点的振幅可以通过考虑事件发生的两种主要方式来近似表示：P(1 到 3) * P(2 到 4) 和 P(1 到 4) * P(2 到 3)，如上所示。根据 1、2、3 和 4 点的相对位置，干涉程度会有所不同。

FIGURE 70. Photons at points 1 and 2 in space-time have an amplitude to arrive at points 3 and 4 in space-time that is approximated by considering two main ways the event could happen: P(1 to 3) * P(2 to 4) and P(1 to 4) * P(2 to 3), shown above. Depending on the relative locations of points 1, 2, 3, and 4, there are varying degrees of interference.

图71.当点 4 和点3 汇聚时，两条箭头——P(1 到 3) * P(2 到 3) 和 P(2 到 3) * P(1 到 3)——长度和方向都相同。当它们相加时，总是会“对齐”，形成一条长度是单独一条箭头两倍的箭头，并且形成一个面积是其四倍的正方形。因此，光子倾向于到达时空中的同一点。光子数量越多，这种效应就越显著。这就是激光器的工作原理。

FIGURE 71. When points 4 and 3 are made to converge, the two arrows—P(1 to 3) * P(2 to 3) and P(2 to 3) * P(1 to 3)—are identical in length and direction. When they are added they always “line up” and form an arrow with twice the length of either arrow alone, with a square four times as large. Thus photons tend to go to the same point in space-time. This effect is magnified even more by more photons. This is the basis of a laser’s operation.

这会产生一系列实际影响。我们可以说，光子倾向于处于相同的状态，或者说“态”（即在空间中寻找该状态的振幅变化方式）。如果原子周围已经存在一些处于原子可以发射光子的状态的光子，那么原子发射光子的概率就会增加。这种“受激辐射”现象是由爱因斯坦在提出光子模型并建立量子理论时发现的。激光的工作原理正是基于这种现象。

This results in a number of practical effects. We can say that photons tend to get into the same condition, or “state” (the way the amplitude to find one varies in space). The chance that an atom emits a photon is enhanced if some photons (in a state that the atom can emit into) are already present. This phenomenon of “stimulated emission” was discovered by Einstein when he launched the quantum theory proposing the photon model of light. Lasers work on the basis of this phenomenon.

图72.如果两个电子（具有相同的极化方向）试图到达时空中的同一点，由于极化效应，干涉结果总是负的：两个相同的箭头——E(1 到 3) * E (2 到 3) 和 E(2 到 3) * E(1 到 3)——相互抵消，最终形成一个长度为零的箭头。两个电子不愿占据时空中同一位置的现象被称为“泡利不相容原理”，它解释了宇宙中原子种类繁多的原因。

FIGURE 72. If two electrons (with the same polarization) try to go to the same point in space-time, the interference is always negative because of the effects of polarization: the two identical arrows—E(1 to 3) * E(2 to 3) and E(2 to 3) * E(1 to 3)—are subtracted to make a final arrow of no length. The aversion of two electrons to occupy the same place in space-time is called the “Exclusion Principle,” and accounts for the great variety of atoms in the universe.

如果我们用我们模拟的自旋为零的电子进行同样的比较，也会发生同样的情况。但在现实世界中，电子是极化的，情况就截然不同了：两个箭头 E(1 到 3) * E(2 到 4) 和 E(1 到 4) * E(2 到 3) 会相互抵消——其中一个箭头在相加之前会旋转 180°。当点 3 和点 4 相同时，这两个箭头的长度和方向相同，因此在相减时相互抵消（见图72）。这意味着电子与光子不同，它们不喜欢聚集到同一个位置；它们像躲避瘟疫一样互相排斥——两个极化方向相同的电子可以处于时空中的同一点——这被称为“不相容原理”。

If we made the same comparison with our fake, spin-zero electrons, the same thing would happen. But in the real world, where electrons are polarized, something very different happens: the two arrows, E(1 to 3) * E(2 to 4) and E(1 to 4) * E(2 to 3), are subtracted—one of them is turned 180° before they are added. When points 3 and 4 are the same, the two arrows have the same length and direction and thus cancel out when they are subtracted (see Fig. 72). That means electrons, unlike photons, do not like to go to the same place; they avoid each other like the plague—no two electrons with the same polarization can be at the same point in space-time—it’s called the “exclusion principle.”

事实证明，这种不相容原理正是原子化学性质多样性的根源。一个质子与围绕它运动的一个电子交换光子，就构成氢原子。同一个原子核内的两个质子分别与两个电子（极化方向相反）交换光子，就构成氦原子。你看，化学家们有一种复杂的计数方法：他们不说“一、二、三、四、五个质子”，而是说“氢、氦、锂、铍、硼”。

This exclusion principle turns out to be the origin of the great variety of chemical properties of the atoms. One proton exchanging photons with one electron dancing around it is called a hydrogen atom. Two protons in the same nucleus exchanging photons with two electrons (polarized in opposite directions) is called a helium atom. You see, the chemists have a complicated way of counting: instead of saying “one, two, three, four, five protons,” they say, “hydrogen, helium, lithium, beryllium, boron.”

电子只有两种极化状态，因此，在一个原子核内有三个质子并与三个电子交换光子的原子（这种情况称为锂原子）中，第三个电子比其他两个电子（它们占据了最近的可用空间）离原子核更远，交换的光子也更少。这使得电子很容易在其他原子的光子作用下脱离原子核。大量这样的原子彼此靠近时，很容易失去各自的第三个电子，形成一片在原子间游动的电子海。这片电子海会对任何微小的电场力（光子）做出反应，产生电流——我描述的就是锂金属的导电现象。氢原子和氦原子不会将电子转移给其他原子，它们是“绝缘体”。

There are only two states of polarization available to electrons, so in an atom with three protons in the nucleus exchanging photons with three electrons—a condition called a lithium atom—the third electron is farther away from the nucleus than the other two (which have used up the nearest available space), and exchanges fewer photons. This causes the electron to easily break away from its own nucleus under the influence of photons from other atoms. A large number of such atoms close together easily lose their individual third electrons to form a sea of electrons swimming around from atom to atom. This sea of electrons reacts to any small electrical force (photons), generating a current of electrons—I am describing lithium metal conducting electricity. Hydrogen and helium atoms do not lose their electrons to other atoms. They are “insulators.”

所有原子——超过一百种不同的原子——都由一定数量的质子与相同数量的电子交换光子构成。它们的聚集模式复杂多样，赋予了它们极其丰富的性质：有些是金属，有些是绝缘体，有些是气体，有些是晶体；有柔软的，有坚硬的，有颜色的，有透明的——如此丰富多彩、令人兴奋的景象，源于原子间的不相容原理和原子核的重复。并且反复出现三个非常简单的动作 P(A 到 B)、E(A 到 B) 和j。（如果世界上的电子没有极化，所有原子的性质都会非常相似：电子都会聚集在一起，靠近自身原子核，并且不容易被其他原子吸引而发生化学反应。）

All the atoms—more than one hundred different kinds—are made up of a certain number of protons exchanging photons with the same number of electrons. The patterns in which they gather are complicated and offer an enormous variety of properties: some are metals, some are insulators, some are gases, others are crystals; there are soft things, hard things, colored things, and transparent things—a terrific cornucopia of variety and excitement that comes from the exclusion principle and the repetition again and again and again of the three very simple actions P(A to B), E(A to B), and j. (If the electrons in the world were unpolarized, all the atoms would have very similar properties: the electrons would all cluster together, close to the nucleus of their own atom, and would not be easily attracted to other atoms to make chemical reactions.)

你或许会好奇，如此简单的动作如何能造就如此复杂的世界。这是因为我们所见的种种现象，都是无数光子交换和干涉相互作用的结果。了解这三种基本作用仅仅是分析任何真实情况的第一步，因为其中涉及的光子交换数量之多，根本无法计算——我们只能通过经验来判断哪些可能性更为重要。因此，我们发明了诸如“折射率”、“可压缩性”或“价态”之类的概念，以便在背后存在大量细节的情况下进行近似计算。这好比了解国际象棋的规则——规则简单易懂——与真正精通国际象棋截然不同，后者需要理解每个棋局的特点和各种情况的本质——这要复杂得多，也更难。

You might wonder how such simple actions could produce such a complex world. It’s because phenomena we see in the world are the result of an enormous intertwining of tremendous numbers of photon exchanges and interferences. Knowing the three fundamental actions is only a very small beginning toward analyzing any real situation, where there is such a multitude of photon exchanges going on that it is impossible to calculate—experience has to be gained as to which possibilities are more important. Thus we invent such ideas as “index of refraction” or “compressibility” or “valence” to help us calculate in an approximate way when there’s an enormous amount of detail going on underneath. It’s analogous to knowing the rules of chess—which are fundamental and simple—compared to being able to play chess well, which involves understanding the character of each position and the nature of various situations—which is much more advanced and difficult.

研究诸如铁（含26个质子）为何具有磁性而铜（含29个质子）为何不具有磁性，或者为什么一种气体透明而另一种气体不透明等问题的物理学分支被称为“固态物理学”、“液态物理学”或“诚实物理学”。而发现这三个简单现象（最容易的部分）的物理学分支则被称为“基础物理学”——我们借用了这个名字，好让其他物理学家感到不自在！如今最有趣的问题——当然也是最实际的问题——显然都属于固态物理学。但有人说过，没有什么比好的理论更实用，而量子电动力学理论绝对是一个好理论！

The branches of physics that deal with questions such as why iron (with 26 protons) is magnetic, while copper (with 29) is not, or why one gas is transparent and another one is not, are called “solid-state physics,” or “liquid-state physics,” or “honest physics.” The branch of physics that found these three simple little actions (the easiest part) is called “fundamental physics”—we stole that name in order to make the other physicists feel uncomfortable! The most interesting problems today—and certainly the most practical problems—are obviously in solid-state physics. But someone said there is nothing so practical as a good theory, and the theory of quantum electrodynamics is definitely a good theory!

最后，我想回到我在第一讲中提到的那个经过精确测量和计算的数字 1.00115965221。这个数字代表电子对外部磁场的响应——也就是所谓的“磁矩”。狄拉克最初推导出计算这个数字的规则时，他使用了 E(A 到 B) 的公式，得到了一个非常简单的答案，我们将其视为 1。电子磁矩的这个初步近似的示意图非常简单——一个电子在时空中从一个位置运动到另一个位置，并与来自磁体的光子耦合（见图73）。

Finally, I would like to return to that number 1.00115965221, the number that I told you about in the first lecture that has been measured and calculated so carefully. The number represents the response of an electron to an external magnetic field—something called the “magnetic moment.” When Dirac first worked out the rules to calculate this number, he used the formula for E(A to B) and got a very simple answer, which we will consider in our units as 1. The diagram for this first approximation of the magnetic moment of an electron is very simple—an electron goes from place to place in space-time and couples with a photon from a magnet (see Fig. 73).

图73.狄拉克计算电子磁矩的图示非常简单。该图所表示的值称为 1。

FIGURE 73. The diagram for Dirac’s calculation of the magnetic moment of an electron is very simple. The value represented by this diagram will be called 1.

几年后人们发现，这个值并非正好是 1，而是略大一些——大约是 1.00116。这个修正值最早由施温格于 1948 年计算得出，即j * j除以 2π。这是由于电子在空间中运动的另一种方式造成的：电子并非直接从一点运动到另一点，而是先运动一段时间，然后突然发射一个光子；接着（天哪！）它又吸收了自己的光子（见图74）。或许这其中有些“不道德”之处，但……电子就能做到！为了计算这种替代方案的箭头，我们需要为光子在时空中所有可能发射和吸收的位置分别绘制一个箭头。因此，将会出现两个额外的 E(A 到 B) 值、一个 P(A 到 B) 值和两个额外的j值，它们相乘即可。学生在研究生二年级的初级量子电动力学课程中会学习如何进行这种简单的计算。

After some years it was discovered that this value was not exactly 1, but slightly more—something like 1.00116. This correction was worked out for the first time in 1948 by Schwinger as j*j divided by 2 pi, and was due to an alternative way the electron can go from place to place: instead of going directly from one point to another, the electron goes along for a while and suddenly emits a photon; then (horrors!) it absorbs its own photon (see Fig. 74). Perhaps there’s something “immoral” about that, but the electron does it! To calculate the arrow for this alternative, we have to make an arrow for every place in space-time that the photon can be emitted and every place it can be absorbed. Thus there will be two extra E(A to B)’s, a P(A to B) and two extra j’s, all multiplied together. Students learn how to do this simple calculation in their elementary quantum electrodynamics course, in their second year of graduate school.

图74。实验室实验表明，电子磁矩的实际值并非 1 ，而是略大于 1。这是因为存在其他路径：电子可以发射一个光子，然后再吸收它——这需要两个额外的 E(A 到 B) 能量、一个 P(A 到 B) 能量以及两个额外的j值。施温格计算出考虑这种路径的调整值为j*j除以 2π。由于这种路径在实验上与电子的原始运动路径（电子从点 1 出发，最终到达点 2）无法区分，因此将两种路径的箭头叠加在一起，从而产生干涉。

FIGURE 74. Laboratory experiments show that the actual value of the magnetic moment of an electron is not 1, but a little bit more. This is because there are alternatives: the electron can emit a photon and then absorb it—requiring two extra E(A to B)’s, a P(A to B), and two extra j’s. Schwinger calculated the adjustment that takes this alternative into account to be j*j divided by 2 pi. Since this alternative is indistinguishable experimentally from the original way the electron can go—an electron starts at point 1 and ends up at point 2—the arrows for the two alternatives are added, and there is interference.

但是等等：实验已经非常精确地测量了电子的行为，以至于我们在计算中不得不考虑其他可能性——电子在四个额外耦合作用下从一个位置移动到另一个位置的所有方式（见图75 ）。电子发射和吸收两个光子的方式有三种。此外，还有一种新的、有趣的可能（如图 75右侧所示）：发射一个光子；它形成一个正电子-电子对，并且——再次强调，如果你愿意……你提出的“道德”异议——电子和正电子湮灭，产生一个新的光子，最终被电子吸收。这种可能性也必须考虑在内！

But wait: experiments have measured the behavior of an electron so accurately that we have to consider still other possibilities in our calculations—all the ways the electron can go from place to place with four extra couplings (see Fig. 75). There are three ways the electron can emit and absorb two photons. There’s also a new, interesting possibility (shown at the right of Fig. 75): one photon is emitted; it makes a positron-electron pair, and—again, if you’ll hold your “moral” objections—the electron and positron annihilate, creating a new photon that is ultimately absorbed by the electron. That possibility also has to be figured in!

两组“独立”的物理学家花了两年时间才计算出下一个项，然后又花了一年时间才发现其中有个错误——实验人员测得的值略有不同，一度让人觉得理论与实验结果第一次出现了矛盾，但事实并非如此：这只是算术错误。两组人怎么会犯同样的错误呢？原来，在计算接近尾声时，两组人互相交流了结果，并修正了计算中的差异，所以他们实际上并非独立进行计算。

It took two “independent” groups of physicists two years to calculate this next term, and then another year to find out there was a mistake—experimenters had measured the value to be slightly different, and it looked for awhile that the theory didn’t agree with experiment for the first time, but no: it was a mistake in arithmetic. How could two groups make the same mistake? It turns out that near the end of the calculation the two groups compared notes and ironed out the differences between their calculations, so they were not really independent.

图75。实验室实验的精度越来越高，以至于需要计算其他方案，这些方案涉及四个额外的耦合（涵盖时空中所有可能的中间点），其中一些方案如图所示。右侧的方案涉及一个光子衰变为一个正电子-电子对（如图 64 所示），该正电子-电子对湮灭形成一个新的光子，该光子最终被电子吸收。

FIGURE 75. Laboratory experiments became so accurate that further alternatives, involving four extra couplings (over all possible intermediate points in space-time), had to be calculated, some of which are shown here. The alternative on the right involves a photon disintegrating into a positron-electron pair (as described in Fig. 64), which annihilates to form a new photon, which is ultimately absorbed by the electron.

包含六个额外j的项涉及更多事件发生的可能方式，我现在就画出其中几种（见图76）。将这种额外的精度纳入磁力线的理论值，花了二十年时间。电子的瞬态过程。与此同时，实验人员进行了更加详细的实验，并在他们的计算结果上增加了几位数字——而理论仍然与这个结果相符。

The term with six extra j’s involves even more possible ways the event can happen, and I’ll draw a few of them for you now (see Fig. 76). It took twenty years to get this extra accuracy figured into the theoretical value of the magnetic moment of an electron. Meanwhile the experimenters made even more detailed experiments and added a few more digits onto their number—and the theory still agreed with it.

图76。目前正在进行计算，以使理论值更加精确。振幅的下一个贡献项代表了所有包含六个额外耦合的可能性，涉及大约 70 个图，此处显示了其中三个。截至 1983 年，理论值为 1.00115965246，最后两位数字的不确定度约为 20；实验值为 1.00115965221，最后一位数字的不确定度约为 4。这种精度相当于将洛杉矶到纽约的距离（超过 3000 英里）测量到头发丝的宽度以内。

FIGURE 76. Calculations are presently going on to make the theoretical value even more accurate. The next contribution to the amplitude, which represents all possibilities with six extra couplings, involves something like 70 diagrams, three of which are shown here. As of 1983, the theoretical number was 1.00115965246, with an uncertainty of about 20 in the last two digits; the experimental number was 1.00115965221, with an uncertainty of about 4 in the last digit. This accuracy is equivalent to measuring the distance from Los Angeles to New York, a distance of over 3,000 miles, to within the width of a human hair.

因此，为了进行计算，我们绘制这些图表，写下它们对应的数学表达式，然后将振幅相加——这是一个简单直接的“照本宣科”式过程。因此，机器可以完成这项工作。现在我们有了超级计算机，已经开始计算包含八个额外j的项。目前，理论值为 1.00115965246；实验值为 1.00115965221，小数点后最后一位有正负 4 的误差。理论值的部分不确定性（小数点后最后一位约 4）是由于计算机的四舍五入造成的；大部分不确定性（约 20）是由于以下原因造成的：j的具体值尚不明确。计算八个额外j的项需要绘制大约九百张图，每张图包含十万个项——这是一项极其复杂的计算——目前正在进行中。

So, to make our calculations we make these diagrams, write down what they correspond to mathematically, and add the amplitudes—a straightforward, “cookbook” process. Therefore, it can be done by machines. Now that we have super-duper computers, we have begun to compute the term with eight extra j’s. At the present time the theoretical number is 1.00115965246; experimentally, it’s 1.00115965221, plus or minus 4 in the last decimal place. Some of the uncertainty in the theoretical value (about 4 in the last decimal place) is due to the computer’s rounding off numbers; most of it (about 20) is due to the fact that the value for j is not exactly known. The term for eight extra j’s involves something like nine hundred diagrams, with a hundred thousand terms each—a fantastic calculation—and it’s being done right now.

我相信再过几年，电子磁矩的理论值和实验值将会被进一步推导出更高的精度。当然，我并不确定这两个值届时是否仍然一致。这一点，只有经过计算和实验才能确定。

I am sure that in a few more years, the theoretical and experimental numbers for the magnetic moment of an electron will be worked out to still more places. Of course, I am not sure whether the two values will still agree. That, one can never tell until one makes the calculation and does the experiments.

所以，我们又回到了我在讲座开始时用来“吓唬”你们的那个数字。我希望你们现在能更好地理解这个数字的意义：它代表着我们不断验证量子电动力学这个奇特理论的非凡程度。

And so we have come full circle to the number I chose to “intimidate” you with at the beginning of these lectures. I hope you understand the significance of this number much better now: it represents the extraordinary degree to which we’ve been constantly checking that the strange theory of quantum electrodynamics is indeed correct.

在这些讲座中，我很高兴地向大家展示，获得如此精确的理论所付出的代价是常识的丧失。我们必须接受一些非常奇特的现象：概率的放大和抑制、光线从镜子的各个部分反射、光线沿非直线路径传播、光子速度超过或低于传统光速、电子逆向运动、光子突然衰变为正电子-电子对等等。我们必须接受这些，才能真正理解自然界在几乎所有我们所见现象背后所进行的真正运作。

Throughout these lectures I have delighted in showing you that the price of gaining such an accurate theory has been the erosion of our common sense. We must accept some very bizarre behavior: the amplification and suppression of probabilities, light reflecting from all parts of a mirror, light travelling in paths other than a straight line, photons going faster or slower than the conventional speed of light, electrons going backwards in time, photons suddenly disintegrating into a positron-electron pair, and so on. That we must do, in order to appreciate what Nature is really doing underneath nearly all the phenomena we see in the world.

除了极化的技术细节之外，我已经向你们描述了我们理解所有这些现象的框架。我们为事件发生的每一种方式绘制振幅，并在通常情况下我们预期将概率相加时将它们相加；在我们预期将概率相乘时，我们将振幅相乘。思考一切振幅术语起初可能因为其抽象性而令人困惑，但过一段时间后，人们就会习惯这种奇特的语言。我们每天观察到的许多现象背后，其实只有三个基本作用：一个可以用简单的耦合数j来描述；另外两个可以用函数 P(A 到 B) 和 E(A 到 B) 来描述，这两个函数密切相关。这就是全部，所有其他物理定律都由此而来。

With the exception of technical details of polarization, I have described to you the framework by which we understand all these phenomena. We draw amplitudes for every way an event can happen and add them when we would have expected to add probabilities under ordinary circumstances; we multiply amplitudes when we would have expected to multiply probabilities. Thinking of everything in terms of amplitudes may cause difficulties at first because of their abstraction, but after a while, one gets used to this strange language. Underneath so many of the phenomena we see every day are only three basic actions: one is described by the simple coupling number, j; the other two by functions—P(A to B) and E(A to B)—both of which are closely related. That’s all there is to it, and from it all the rest of the laws of physics come.

不过，在结束本次讲座之前，我想补充几点。即使不涉及偏振这个技术细节，也能理解量子电动力学的精神和本质。但我相信，如果我不解释一下我省略的内容，大家都会感到不自在。事实上，光子有四种不同的偏振态，它们与空间和时间的方向存在几何关系。因此，光子分别沿 X、Y、Z 和 T 方向偏振。（也许你们听说过光只有两种偏振态——例如，沿 Z 方向运动的光子可以垂直于 Z 方向偏振，即沿 X 或 Y 方向偏振。没错：在光子长距离运动且速度接近光速的情况下，Z 和 T 分量的振幅会完全抵消。但对于在原子中质子和电子之间传播的虚光子而言，T 分量才是最重要的。）

However, before I finish this lecture, I would like to make a few additional remarks. One can understand the spirit and character of quantum electrodynamics without including this technical detail of polarization. But I’m sure you’ll all feel uncomfortable unless I say something about what I’ve been leaving out. Photons, it turns out, come in four different varieties, called polarizations, that are related geometrically to the directions of space and time. Thus there are photons polarized in the X, Y, Z, and T directions. (Perhaps you have heard somewhere that light comes in only two states of polarization—for example, a photon going in the Z direction can be polarized at right angles, either in the X or Y direction. Well, you guessed it: in situations where the photon goes a long distance and appears to go at the speed of light, the amplitudes for the Z and T terms exactly cancel out. But for virtual photons going between a proton and an electron in an atom, it is the T component that is the most important.)

类似地，电子可以处于四种状态之一，这些状态也与几何结构相关，但关系更为微妙。我们可以将这些状态称为状态 1、2、3 和 4。计算电子在时空中从 A 点到 B 点的振幅变得稍微复杂一些，因为我们现在可以提出这样的问题：“在 A 点以状态 2 释放的电子到达 B 点时的状态 3 的振幅是多少？” 十六种可能的组合——来自……电子在 A 点可能处于的四种不同状态，以及它在 B 点可能处于的四种不同状态——这四种不同的状态与我之前提到的 E(A 到 B) 的公式之间存在着简单的数学关系。

In a similar manner, an electron can be in one of four conditions that are also related to geometry, but in a somewhat more subtle manner. We can call these conditions 1, 2, 3, and 4. Calculating the amplitude for an electron going from point A to point B in space-time becomes somewhat more complicated, because we can now ask questions such as, “What is the amplitude that an electron liberated in condition 2 at the point A arrives in condition 3 at the point B?” The sixteen possible combinations—coming from the four different conditions an electron can start in at A and the four different conditions it can end up in at B—are related in a simple mathematical way to the formula for that E(A to B) I told you about.

对于光子而言，无需进行此类修改。因此，在 A 点沿 X 方向偏振的光子在 B 点仍然沿 X 方向偏振，到达时振幅为 P(A 到 B)。

For a photon, no such modification is necessary. Thus a photon polarized in the X direction at A will still be polarized in the X direction at B, arriving with the amplitude P(A to B).

极化会产生大量不同的耦合方式。例如，我们可以问：“处于状态 2 的电子吸收沿 X 方向极化的光子，从而转变为处于状态 3 的电子，其振幅是多少？”并非所有极化电子和光子的组合都会耦合，但那些能够耦合的组合，其耦合振幅均为 7，只是有时会使箭头额外旋转 90° 的倍数。

Polarization produces a large number of different possible couplings. We could ask, for example, “What is the amplitude that an electron in condition 2 absorbs a photon polarized in the X direction and thereby turns into an electron in condition 3?” All the possible combinations of polarized electrons and photons do not couple, but those that do, do so with the same amplitude 7, but sometimes with an additional turn of the arrow by some multiple of 90°.

这些不同类型极化和耦合性质的可能性，都可以用非常优雅的方式，从量子电动力学原理和两个附加假设中推导出来：1）即使实验装置朝另一个方向旋转，实验结果也不会受到影响；2）即使装置位于以任意速度运动的宇宙飞船中，结果也不会有任何不同。（这就是相对性原理。）

These possibilities for the different kinds of polarization and the nature of the couplings can all be deduced in a very elegant and beautiful manner from the principles of quantum electrodynamics and two further assumptions: 1) the results of an experiment are not affected if the apparatus with which you are making experiments is turned in some other direction, and 2) it also doesn’t make any difference if the apparatus is in a spaceship moving at some arbitrary speed. (This is the principle of relativity.)

这种简洁而普遍的分析表明，每个粒子都必然属于某一类可能的极化状态，我们称之为自旋0、自旋1/2、自旋1、自旋3/2、自旋2等等。不同类别的粒子表现出不同的性质。自旋0粒子是最简单的——它只有一个分量，并且实际上完全没有极化。（我们在本讲中讨论的模拟电子和光子都是自旋0粒子。迄今为止，尚未发现任何基本的自旋0粒子。）真实的电子是自旋1/2粒子的一个例子，真实的光子是自旋1的一个例子。粒子。自旋为 1/2 和自旋为 1 的粒子都由四个分量组成。其他类型的粒子则包含更多分量，例如自旋为 2 的粒子，它有十个分量。

This elegant and general analysis shows that every particle must be in one or another class of possible polarizations, which we call spin 0, spin 1/2, spin 1, spin 3/2, spin 2, and so on. The different classes behave in different ways. A spin 0 particle is the simplest—it has just one component, and is not effectively polarized at all. (The fake electrons and photons that we have been considering in this lecture are spin 0 particles. So far, no fundamental spin 0 particles have been found.) A real electron is an example of a spin 1/2 particle, and a real photon is an example of a spin 1 particle. Both spin 1/2 and spin 1 particles have four components. The other types would have more components, such as spin 2 particles, with ten components.

我说过相对论和极化之间的联系既简单又优雅，但我并不确定自己能否用简洁优雅的方式解释清楚！（至少需要再讲一节课才能解释清楚。）虽然极化的细节对于理解量子电动力学的精神和本质并非至关重要，但它们对于任何实际过程的正确计算当然都必不可少，而且往往会产生深远的影响。

I said that the connection between relativity and polarization is simple and elegant, but I’m not sure I can explain it simply and elegantly! (It would take me at least one additional lecture to do it.) Although the details of polarization are not essential to understanding the spirit and character of quantum electrodynamics, they are, of course, essential to the correct calculation of any real process, and often have profound effects.

在这些讲座中，我们一直专注于电子和光子在极小距离内相对简单的相互作用，其中只涉及少数粒子。但我想就这些相互作用在更大的世界中如何表现做一些说明，在更大的世界中，大量的光子在交换。在如此大的尺度上，箭头的计算变得非常复杂。

In these lectures we have been concentrating on relatively simple interactions between electrons and photons at very small distances, in which only a few particles are involved. But I would like to make one or two remarks about how these interactions appear in the larger world, where very, very large numbers of photons are being exchanged. On such a large scale, the calculation of arrows gets very complicated.

然而，有些情况并不难分析。例如，在某些情况下，光源发射光子的振幅与之前是否已经发射过其他光子无关。这种情况可能发生在光源质量非常大时（例如原子核），或者当大量电子都以相同方向运动时，例如广播电台天线中的上下运动或电磁铁线圈中的绕圈运动。在这种情况下，会发射大量光子，而且所有光子都完全相同。在这种环境下，电子吸收光子的振幅与它自身或其他电子之前是否吸收过其他光子无关。因此，电子的全部行为都可以用这个吸收光子的振幅来描述，而这个振幅只取决于电子在时空中的位置。物理学家使用普通的电子吸收光子振幅来描述电子吸收光子的行为。用来描述这种情况的词语是：电子在外部场中运动。

There are, however, some situations that are not so difficult to analyze. There are circumstances, for example, where the amplitude to emit a photon by a source is independent of whether another photon has been emitted. This can happen when the source is very heavy (the nucleus of an atom), or when a very large number of electrons are all moving the same way, such as up and down in the antenna of a broadcasting station or going around in the coils of an electromagnet. Under such circumstances a large number of photons are emitted, all of exactly the same kind. The amplitude of an electron to absorb a photon in such an environment is independent of whether it or any other electron has absorbed other photons before. Therefore its entire behavior can be given by just this amplitude for an electron to absorb a photon, which depends only on the electron’s position in space and time. Physicists use ordinary words to describe this circumstance. They say the electron is moving in an external field.

物理学家用“场”一词来描述一个取决于空间和时间位置的物理量。空气温度就是一个很好的例子：它会随着测量地点和时间的变化而变化。当我们考虑偏振效应时，场就有了更多分量。（有四个分量——分别对应于吸收光子不同偏振态（X、Y、Z、T）的振幅——技术上称为矢量和标量电磁势。经典物理学从这些分量的组合中推导出更方便的分量，称为电场和磁场。）

Physicists use the word “field” to describe a quantity that depends on position in space and time. Temperatures in the air provide a good example: they vary according to where and when you make your measurements. When we take polarization into account, there are more components to the field. (There are four components—corresponding to the amplitude to absorb each of the different kinds of polarization (X, Y, Z, T) the photon might be in—technically called the vector and scalar electromagnetic potentials. From combinations of these, classical physics derives more convenient components called the electric and magnetic fields.)

在电场和磁场变化足够缓慢的情况下，电子长距离运动所需的振幅取决于其行进路径。正如我们之前在光的例子中看到的，最重要的路径是那些相邻路径振幅夹角几乎相同的路径。因此，粒子不一定沿直线运动。

In a situation where the electric and magnetic fields are varying slowly enough, the amplitude for an electron to travel over a very long distance depends on the path it takes. As we saw earlier in the case of light, the most important paths are the ones where the angles of the amplitudes from nearby paths are nearly the same. The result is that the particle doesn’t necessarily go in a straight line.

这就把我们带回到了经典物理学，它假设存在场，电子在其中运动，使得某个量最小。（物理学家称这个量为“作用量”，并将这条规则表述为“最小作用量原理”。）这是量子电动力学规则如何在大尺度上产生现象的一个例子。我们可以从这里向许多方向展开讨论，但我们必须在某些方面限制这些讲座的范围。我只是想提醒大家，我们在大尺度上观察到的效应和我们在小尺度上观察到的奇异现象，都是由电子和光子的相互作用产生的，最终都可以用量子电动力学理论来描述。

This brings us all the way back to classical physics, which supposes that there are fields and that electrons move through them in such a way as to make a certain quantity least. (Physicists call this quantity “action” and formulate this rule as the “principle of least action.”) This is one example of how the rules of quantum electrodynamics produce phenomena on a large scale. We could expand in many directions from here, but we have to limit the scope of these lectures somewhere. I just wanted to remind you that the effects that we see on a large scale and the strange phenomena we see on a small scale are both produced by the interaction of electrons and photons, and are all described, ultimately, by the theory of quantum electrodynamics.

1.关于这种情况的完整描述非常有趣：如果A和B处的探测器并非完美，只能偶尔探测到光子，那么会出现三种不同的最终状态：1）A和D处的探测器都失效；2）B和D处的探测器都失效；3）只有D处的探测器失效，A和B保持不变（它们仍处于初始状态）。前两种情况的概率计算方法与上文所述相同（只是多了一步——由于探测器并非完美，A[或B]处的探测器失效的概率需要缩小）。当只有D处的探测器失效时，我们无法区分这两种情况，自然界会通过引入干涉来“捉弄”我们——这与没有探测器时的结果相同（只是最终的箭头会因探测器未失效而缩小）。最终结果是一个混合体，是所有三种情况的简单叠加（见图51）。随着探测器可靠性的提高，我们受到的干扰也会减少。

1 The complete story on this situation is very interesting: if the detectors at A and B are not perfect, and detect photons only some of the time, then there are three distinguishable final conditions: 1) the detectors at A and D go off; 2) the detectors at B and D go off, and 3) the detector at D goes off alone, with A and B unchanged (they are left in their initial state). The probabilities for the first two events are calculated in the way explained above (except that there will be an extra step—a shrink for the probability that the detector at A [or B] goes off, since the detectors are not perfect). When D goes off alone, we can’t separate the two cases, and Nature plays with us by bringing in interference—the same peculiar answer we would have had if there were no detectors (except that the final arrow is shrunk by the amplitude that the detectors do not go off). The final result is a mixture, the simple sum of all three cases (see Fig. 51). As the reliability of the detectors increases, we get less interference.

2在这些讲座中，我将在一维空间（沿 x 轴）中绘制一个点的位置。要在三维空间中定位一个点，需要设置一个“房间”，并测量该点到地面以及到相邻两面墙（彼此垂直）的距离。这三个测量值可以标记为 X1、 Y1和 Z1。从该点到第二个点（其测量值为 X2、Y2 和 Z2）的实际距离可以使用“三维勾股定理”计算：该实际距离的平方为

2 In these lectures, I am plotting a point’s location in space in one dimension, along the x-axis. To locate a point in three-dimensional space, a “room” has to be set up, and the distance of the point from the floor and from each of two adjacent walls (all at right angles to each other) has to be measured. These three measurements can be labeled Xl Y1 and Z1. The actual distance from this point to a second point with measurements X2, Y2, Z2 can be calculated using a “three-dimensional Pythagorean Theorem”: the square of this actual distance is

（X 2 – X 1）2 + （Y 2 – Y 1）2 + （Z 2 – Z 1）2。

(X2 – X1)2 + (Y2 – Y1)2 + (Z2 – Z1)2.

该值超过时间差的平方——

The excess of this over the time difference, squared—

( X₂ – X₁ ) ² + (Y₂ – Y₁ ) ² + (Z₂ – Z₁ ) ² – ( T₂ – T₁ ) ²

(X2 – X1)2 + (Y2 – Y1)2 + (Z2 – Z1)2 – (T2 – T1)2

——有时被称为“区间”，或简称I，爱因斯坦的相对论指出，P(A 到 B) 必须取决于这个组合。P(A 到 B) 的最终箭头的大部分贡献正如你所预期的那样——距离差等于时间差（即I为零时）。但除此之外，当I不为零时，还有一部分贡献，它与I成反比：当I大于零时（光速大于c ），它指向 3 点钟方向；当 I 小于零时，它指向9 点钟方向。在许多情况下，这些后一部分贡献会相互抵消（参见图 56）。

—is sometimes called “the Interval,” or I, and is the combination that Einstein’s theory of relativity says that P(A to B) must depend on. Most of the contribution to the final arrow for P(A to B) is just where you would expect it—where the difference in distance is equal to the difference in time (that is, when I is zero). But in addition; there is a contribution when I is not zero, that is inversely proportional to I: it points in the direction of 3 o’clock when I is more than zero (when light is going faster than c), and points toward 9 o’clock when I is less than zero. These later contributions cancel out in many circumstances (see Fig. 56).

3 E(A 到 B) 的公式很复杂，但有一种有趣的方法来解释它的含义。E(A 到 B) 可以表示为电子在时空中从 A 点到 B 点的各种不同路径的总和（见图57）：电子可以进行“一跳飞行”，直接从 A 点到达 B 点；它可以进行“两跳飞行”，在中间点 C 停留；它可以进行“三跳飞行”，在点 D 和 E 停留，等等。在这种分析中，每次“跳跃”（从点 F 到点 G）的振幅为 P(F 到 G)，与光子从点 F 到点 G 的振幅相同。每次“停留”的振幅用 n² 表示，n是我之前提到的用于使计算结果正确的那个数。

3 The formula for E(A to B) is complicated, but there is an interesting way to explain what it amounts to. E(A to B) can be represented as a giant sum of a lot of different ways an electron could go from point A to point B in space-time (see Fig. 57): the electron could take a “one-hop flight,” going directly from A to B; it could take a “two-hop flight,” stopping at an intermediate point C; it could take a “three-hop flight,” stopping at points D and E, and so on. In such an analysis, the amplitude for each “hop”—from one point F to another point G—is P(F to G), the same as the amplitude for a photon to go from a point F to a point G. The amplitude for each “stop” is represented by n2, n being the same number I mentioned before which we used to make our calculations come out right.

因此，E(A 到 B) 的公式是一系列项：P(A 到 B) [“一跳”航班] + P(A 到 C)* n 2 *P(C 到 B) [“两跳”航班，在 C 停留] + P(A 到 D)* n 2 *P(D 到 E) * n 2 *P(E 到 B) [“三跳”航班，在 D 和 E 停留] + … 对于所有可能的中间点C、D、E 等。

The formula for E(A to B) is thus a series of terms: P(A to B) [the “one-hop” flight] + P(A to C)*n2*P(C to B) [“two-hop” flights, stopping at C] + P(A to D)*n2*P(D to E) * n2*P(E to B) [“three-hop” flights, stopping at D and E] + … for all possible intermediate points C, D, E, and so on.

注意，当n增大时，非直接路径对最终箭头的贡献也随之增大。当n为零时（例如光子的情况），所有包含n 的项都会消失（因为它们也等于零），只剩下第一项，即 P(A 到 B)。因此，E(A 到 B) 和 P(A 到 B) 密切相关。

Note that when n increases, the nondirect paths make a greater contribution to the final arrow. When n is zero (as for the photon), all terms with an n drop out (because they are also equal to zero), leaving only the first term, which is P(A to B). Thus E(A to B) and P(A to B) are closely related.

4这个数字，即发射或吸收光子的振幅，有时被称为粒子的“电荷”。

4 This number, the amplitude to emit or absorb a photon, is sometimes called the “charge” of a particle.

5如果我考虑了电子极化的影响，“第二条路径”的箭头就需要“减去”——旋转180°后再加上去。（稍后会在本讲中详细讨论这一点。）

5 Had I included the effects of the polarization of the electron, the “second-way” arrow would have been “subtracted”—turned 180° and added. (More on this comes later in this lecture.)

6.这些更复杂方式的实验最终条件与更简单方式相同——电子从点 1 和 2 出发，最终到达点 3 和 4——因此我们无法区分这些方案与前两种方案。所以，我们必须将这两种方案的箭头添加到之前讨论的两种方案中。

6 The final conditions of the experiment for these more complicated ways are the same as for the simpler ways—electrons start at points 1 and 2 and end up at points 3 and 4—so we cannot distinguish between these alternatives and the first two. Therefore we must add the arrows for these two ways to the two ways just previously considered.

7这种在实验的初始或最终条件下从未真正出现的交换光子有时被称为“虚光子”。

7 Such an exchanged photon that never really appears in the initial or final conditions of the experiment is sometimes called a “virtual photon.”

1931年，狄拉克提出了“反电子”的真实存在；次年，卡尔·安德森通过实验发现了它们，并将其命名为“正电子”。如今，正电子很容易制造（例如，通过使两个光子相互碰撞），并且可以在磁场中保存数周。

8 Dirac proposed the reality of “anti-electrons” in 1931; in the following year, Carl Anderson found them experimentally and called them “positrons.” Today, positrons can be easily made (for example, by making two photons collide with each other) and kept for weeks in a magnetic field.

9光子交换的振幅为 (– j ) * P(AB) * j —两种耦合以及光子从一个位置到另一个位置的振幅。质子与光子耦合的振幅为 – j。

9 The amplitude for the photon exchange is (–j) * P(A-B) * j—two couplings and the amplitude for a photon to go from place to place. The amplitude for a proton to have a coupling with a photon is –j.

10弧的半径显然取决于每段箭头的长度，而箭头的长度最终取决于玻璃原子中电子散射光子的振幅 S。该半径可以通过计算涉及众多光子交换的三种基本作用的公式，并将振幅相加来计算。这是一个非常困难的问题，但对于相对简单的物质，半径的计算已经取得了相当大的成功，并且利用量子电动力学的思想，人们对不同物质间半径的变化也得到了相当充分的理解。然而，必须指出的是，对于像玻璃这样复杂的物质，从​​未真正进行过从第一性原理出发的直接计算。在这种情况下，半径是通过实验确定的。对于玻璃，实验已经确定，当光线垂直照射到玻璃上时，半径约为 0.2。

10 The radius of the arc evidently depends on the length of the arrow for each section, which is ultimately determined by the amplitude S that an electron in an atom of glass scatters a photon. This radius can be calculated using the formulas for the three basic actions for the multitude of photon exchanges involved and summing up the amplitudes. It is a very difficult problem, but the radius has been calculated for relatively simple substances with considerable success, and the variation of the radius from substance to substance is fairly well understood using these ideas of quantum electrodynamics. It must be said, however, that no direct calculation from first principles for a substance as complex as glass has ever actually been done. In such cases, the radius is determined by experiment. For glass, it has been determined from experiment that the radius is approximately 0.2 (when the light shines directly onto the glass at right angles)

11.每一截面反射的箭头（构成一个“圆”）的长度，都与使最终透射箭头看起来旋转角度更大的箭头长度相同。因此，材料的偏反射与其折射率之间存在某种关系。

11 Each of the arrows for reflection by a section (that form a “circle”) has the same length as each of the arrows that make the final arrow from transmission appear to turn more. Thus there is a relationship between the partial reflection of a material and its index of refraction.

看起来最后一个箭头的长度超过了 1，这意味着穿过玻璃的光比进入玻璃的光多！之所以看起来是这样，是因为我忽略了光子向下进入一个区域、另一个光子散射到另一个区域、然后第三个光子散射回穿过玻璃的振幅——以及其他更复杂的可能性——这些因素导致小箭头弯曲，并将最后一个箭头的长度保持在 0.92 到 1 之间（因此，光被玻璃层反射或透射的总概率始终为 100%）。

It appears that the final arrow has become longer than 1, which means that more light comes out through the glass than went into it! It looks that way because I disregarded the amplitudes for a photon to go down to one section, a new photon to scatter up to another section, and then a third photon to scatter back down through the glass—and other, more complicated possibilities—which result in the little arrows curving around and keeping the length of the final arrow between 0.92 and 1 (so the total probability of light being reflected or transmitted by the layer of glass is always 100%).

4

4

未完成事项

Loose Ends

我将把这次讲座分为两部分。首先，我将讨论量子电动力学理论本身存在的问题，假设世界上只有电子和光子。然后，我将讨论量子电动力学与物理学其他领域的联系。

I am going to divide this lecture into two parts. First, I am going to talk about problems associated with the theory of quantum electrodynamics itself, supposing that all there is in the world is electrons and photons. Then I will talk about the relation of quantum electrodynamics to the rest of physics.

量子电动力学理论最令人震惊的特征是其复杂的振幅框架，你可能会认为这预示着某种问题！然而，物理学家们已经研究振幅超过五十年，并且早已习以为常。此外，我们能够观测到的所有新粒子和新现象都与从该振幅框架中推导出的所有内容完美契合。在这个框架中，事件发生的概率是最终箭头长度的平方，而最终箭头的长度则由以各种奇特方式组合箭头（例如干涉等）决定。因此，这个振幅框架在实验上没有任何疑问：你可以对振幅的意义（如果它们真的有意义的话）提出各种哲学上的疑问，但由于物理学是一门实验科学，而该框架与实验结果相符，因此到目前为止，它对我们来说已经足够好了。

The most shocking characteristic of the theory of quantum electrodynamics is the crazy framework of amplitudes, which you might think indicates problems of some sort! However, physicists have been fiddling around with amplitudes for more than fifty years now, and have gotten very used to it. Furthermore, all the new particles and new phenomena that we are able to observe fit perfectly with everything that can be deduced from such a framework of amplitudes, in which the probability of an event is the square of a final arrow whose length is determined by combining arrows in funny ways (with interferences, and so on). So this framework of amplitudes has no experimental doubt about it: you can have all the philosophical worries you want as to what the amplitudes mean (if, indeed, they mean anything at all), but because physics is an experimental science and the framework agrees with experiment, it’s good enough for us so far.

量子电动力学理论中存在一系列问题，这些问题与改进计算所有小箭头之和的方法有关——在不同情况下，可以使用各种不同的分析技术——研究生需要三到四年的时间才能掌握这些技术。由于这些都是技术性问题，我在这里就不赘述了。关键在于不断改进分析技术，以便更好地理解理论在不同情况下的真正含义。

There is a set of problems associated with the theory of quantum electrodynamics that has to do with improving the method of calculating the sum of all the little arrows—various techniques that are available in different circumstances—that take the graduate students three or four years to master. Since they are technical problems, I am not going to discuss them here. It’s just a matter of continuously improving the techniques for analyzing what the theory really has to say in different circumstances.

但还有一个问题是量子电动力学理论本身特有的，这个问题花了二十年才得以解决。它与理想电子和光子以及数字n和j有关。

But there is one additional problem that is characteristic of the theory of quantum electrodynamics itself, which took twenty years to overcome. It has to do with ideal electrons and photons and the numbers n and j.

图77。当我们计算电子在时空中从一点运动到另一点的振幅时，我们使用直接路径的 E(A 到 B) 公式。（然后我们进行“修正”，包括一个或多个光子的发射和吸收。）E(A 到 B) 取决于 (X₂ – X₁ ) 、 ( T₂ – T₁ )和n，n 是一个代入公式以使结果正确的数值。n被称为“理想”电子的“静止质量”，无法通过实验测量，因为实际电子的静止质量m包含了所有“修正”。计算E(A 到 B) 中使用的n值存在一定的困难，这个问题花了二十年才得以解决。

FIGURE 77. When we calculate the amplitude for an electron to go from point to point in space-time, we use the formula for E(A to B) for the direct path. (Then we make “corrections” that include one or more photons being emitted and absorbed.) E(A to B) depends on (X2 – X1), (T2 – T1) and n, a number we stick into the formula to make the answer come out right. The number n is called the “rest-mass” of an “ideal” electron, and cannot be measured experimentally because the rest-mass of a real electron, m, includes all the “corrections.” There is a certain difficulty in calculating the n to be used in E(A to B), that took twenty years to overcome.

如果电子是理想的，并且在时空中仅沿直线路径（如图 77左侧所示）运动，那么就不会有问题：n就是电子的质量（我们可以通过观测确定），j就是它的“电荷”（振幅）。电子与光子耦合所需的时间）。也可以通过实验确定。

If electrons were ideal, and went from point to point in space-time only by the direct path (shown at the left in Fig. 77), then there would be no problem: n would simply be the mass of an electron (which we can determine by observation), and j would simply be its “charge” (the amplitude for the electron to couple with a photon). It can also be determined by experiment.

但理想电子并不存在。我们在实验室中观测到的质量是真实电子的质量，它会不时地发射和吸收自身的光子，因此取决于耦合强度j。我们观测到的电荷是真实电子和真实光子之间的电荷——它们可以不时地形成电子-正电子对——因此取决于 E(A 到 B)，而 E(A 到 B) 又与n相关（见图78）。由于电子的质量和电荷会受到这些以及所有其他因素的影响，因此实验测得的电子质量m和电荷e与我们在计算中使用的数值n和j 并不相同。

But no such ideal electrons exist. The mass we observe in the laboratory is that of a real electron, which emits and absorbs its own photons from time to time, and therefore depends on the amplitude for coupling, j. And the charge we observe is between a real electron and a real photon—which can form an electron-positron pair from time to time—and therefore depends on E (A to B), which involves n (see Fig. 78). Since the mass and charge of an electron are affected by these and all other alternatives, the experimentally measured mass, m, and the experimentally measured charge, e, of the electron are different from the numbers we use in our calculations, n and j.

图78。电子与光子耦合的实验测量振幅，即神秘的数值e，是一个由实验确定的数值，它包含了光子在时空中从一点传播到另一点的所有“修正”，其中两个修正项如图所示。在计算时，我们需要一个数值j ，它不包含这些修正项，而只包含光子直接从一点传播到另一点的情况。计算j的难点与计算 n 的难点类似。

FIGURE 78. The experimentally measured amplitude for an electron to couple with a photon, a mysterious number, e, is a number determined by experiment that includes all the “corrections” for a photon going from point to point in space-time, of which two are shown here. When calculating, we need a number, j, that does not include these corrections, but includes only the photon going directly from point to point. A difficulty exists with computing this j that is similar to the difficulty in computing the value of n.

如果n和j之间，以及m和e之间，存在明确的数学联系，那么这仍然不是问题：我们只需计算出需要从哪些n和j值开始才能得到观测值m和e。 （如果我们的计算结果与m和e不符，我们会调整初始的n和j值，直到它们相符为止。）

If there were a definite mathematical connection between n and j on the one hand, and m and e on the other, there would still be no problem: we would simply calculate what values of n and j we need to start with in order to end up with the observed values, m and e. (If our calculations didn’t agree with m and e, we would jiggle the original n and j around until they did.)

让我们看看我们是如何实际计算m 的。我们写出一系列项，类似于我们之前看到的电子磁矩的级数：第一项没有耦合——只有 E(A 到 B)——代表一个理想电子在时空中直接从一点运动到另一点。第二项有两个耦合，代表一个光子的发射和吸收。接下来是具有四个、六个和八个耦合的项，依此类推（其中一些“修正”如图 77所示）。

Let’s see how we actually calculate m. We write a series of terms that is something like the series we saw for the magnetic moment of the electron: the first term has no couplings—just E (A to B)—and represents an ideal electron going directly from point to point in space-time. The second term has two couplings and represents a photon being emitted and absorbed. Then come terms with four, six, and eight couplings, and so on (some of these “corrections” are shown in Fig. 77).

在计算包含耦合项时，我们必须（一如既往地）考虑所有可能的耦合点，甚至包括两个耦合点重合的情况——它们之间的距离为零。问题在于，当我们尝试计算到距离为零的情况时，方程会崩溃，给出毫无意义的答案——比如无穷大。这在量子电动力学理论刚出现时造成了很大的麻烦。人们尝试计算的每个问题都得到了无穷大！（为了保持数学上的一致性，应该能够计算到距离为零的情况，但正是在这种情况下，没有任何n或j是有意义的；这就是问题的症结所在。）

When calculating terms with couplings, we must consider (as always) all the possible points where couplings can occur, right down to cases where the two coupling points are on top of each other—with zero distance between them. The problem is, when we try to calculate all the way down to zero distance, the equation blows up in our face and gives meaningless answers—things like infinity. This caused a lot of trouble when the theory of quantum electrodynamics first came out. People were getting infinity for every problem they tried to calculate! (One should be able to go down to zero distance in order to be mathematically consistent, but that’s where there is no n or j that makes any sense; that’s where the trouble is.)

与其将所有可能的耦合点都计算到距离为零，不如在耦合点之间的距离非常小时停止计算——比如 10⁻³⁰厘米，比实验中可观测到的任何距离（目前为10⁻¹⁶厘米）小数十亿倍——这样我们就可以确定n和j的具体值，使得计算出的质量与实验中观测到的质量m相符，并且计算出的电荷也与实验中观测到的质量相符。与观测到的电荷e 相符。现在，问题来了：如果其他人来计算，并在不同的距离（例如 10 - 40厘米）处停止计算，那么他们为了得到相同的m和e而需要的n和j值就会不同！

Well, instead of including all possible coupling points down to a distance of zero, if one stops the calculation when the distance between coupling points is very small—say, 10-30 centimeters, billions and billions of times smaller than anything observable in experiment (presently 10-16 centimeters)—then there are definite values for n and j that we can use so that the calculated mass comes out to match the m observed in experiments, and the calculated charge matches the observed charge, e. Now, here’s the catch: if somebody else comes along and stops their calculation at a different distance—say, 10-40 centimeters—their values for n and j needed to get the same m and e come out different!

二十年后，也就是1949年，汉斯·贝特和维克托·魏斯科普夫注意到一个现象：如果两个人分别在不同的距离处停止计算，用相同的m和e值来确定n和j ，然后用各自不同的n和j值来计算另一个问题的答案——当所有项的箭头都包含在内时，他们对这个问题的答案几乎相同！事实上，n和j的计算停止距离越接近于零，对另一个问题的最终答案就越吻合！施温格、友永振一郎和我各自独立地发明了进行精确计算的方法来验证这一现象（我们因此获得了奖项）。人们终于可以用量子电动力学理论进行计算了！

Twenty years later, in 1949, Hans Bethe and Victor Weisskopf noticed something: if two people who stopped at different distances to determine n and j from the same m and e then calculated the answer to some other problem—each using the appropriate but different values for n and j—when all the arrows from all the terms were included, their answers to this other problem came out nearly the same! In fact, the closer to zero distance that the calculations for n and j were stopped, the better the final answers for the other problem would agree! Schwinger, Tomonaga, and I independently invented ways to make definite calculations to confirm that it is true (we got prizes for that). People could finally calculate with the theory of quantum electrodynamics!

因此，似乎只有n和j的值取决于耦合点之间的小距离——这些是理论值，无论如何都无法直接观察到；其他所有可以观察到的因素似乎都不受影响。

So it appears that the only things that depend on the small distances between coupling points are the values for n and j—theoretical numbers that are not directly observable anyway; everything else, which can be observed, seems not to be affected.

我们用来寻找n和j 的这种“障眼法”在技术上被称为“重整化”。但无论这个词听起来多么巧妙，在我看来，这都是一个愚蠢的过程！正是因为不得不诉诸这种故弄玄虚的方法，我们才无法证明量子电动力学理论在数学上是自洽的。令人惊讶的是，到现在为止，这个理论仍然没有被证明是自洽的；我怀疑重整化在数学上是不合理的。可以肯定的是，我们没有一个好的数学方法来描述量子电动力学理论：这么多的词汇来描述这种联系实在太难了。n和j以及m和e之间的运算不符合数学规范。1

The shell game that we play to find n and j is technically called “renormalization.” But no matter how clever the word, it is what I would call a dippy process! Having to resort to such hocus-pocus has prevented us from proving that the theory of quantum electrodynamics is mathematically self-consistent. It’s surprising that the theory still hasn’t been proved self-consistent one way or the other by now; I suspect that renormalization is not mathematically legitimate. What is certain is that we do not have a good mathematical way to describe the theory of quantum electrodynamics: such a bunch of words to describe the connection between n and j and m and e is not good mathematics.1

与观测到的耦合常数e（真实电子发射或吸收真实光子的振幅）相关的，存在一个极其深刻而优美的问题。这是一个简单的数字，实验测得其值接近 -0.08542455。（我的物理学家朋友们可能不认识这个数字，因为他们喜欢记住它的平方倒数：约等于 137.03597，小数点后最后一位的不确定度约为 2。自五十多年前发现以来，它一直是一个谜，所有优秀的理论物理学家都会把这个数字挂在墙上，反复琢磨。）

There is a most profound and beautiful question associated with the observed coupling constant, e—the amplitude for a real electron to emit or absorb a real photon. It is a simple number that has been experimentally determined to be close to –0.08542455. (My physicist friends won’t recognize this number, because they like to remember it as the inverse of its square: about 137.03597 with an uncertainty of about 2 in the last decimal place. It has been a mystery ever since it was discovered more than fifty years ago, and all good theoretical physicists put this number up on their wall and worry about it.)

你肯定很想知道这个耦合常数是怎么来的：它跟π有关吗？还是跟自然对数的底数有关？没人知道。这是物理学最大的谜团之一：一个神奇的数字，人类对此一无所知。你或许会说这是“上帝之手”写下的，而“我们不知道祂是如何挥动笔杆的”。我们知道该如何通过实验精确测量这个数字，但我们不知道该如何通过计算机程序才能得到这个数字——除非偷偷地输入了它！

Immediately you would like to know where this number for a coupling comes from: is it related to pi, or perhaps to the base of natural logarithms? Nobody knows. It’s one of the greatest damn mysteries of physics: a magic number that comes to us with no understanding by man. You might say the “hand of God” wrote that number, and “we don’t know how He pushed His pencil.” We know what kind of a dance to do experimentally to measure this number very accurately, but we don’t know what kind of a dance to do on a computer to make this number come out—without putting it in secretly!

一个合理的理论会认为e是 3 的平方根。大概是 2π 的平方，或者别的什么。关于e到底是多少，人们时不时会提出一些建议，但都无济于事。首先，亚瑟·爱丁顿用纯粹的逻辑证明，物理学家们喜欢的那个数字必须正好是 136，也就是当时的实验值。后来，随着更精确的实验表明这个数字更接近 137，爱丁顿发现他之前的论证中存在一个小错误，并再次用纯粹的逻辑证明，这个数字必须是整数 137！偶尔会有人注意到，π 和 e（自然对数的底数）、2 和 5 的某些组合可以得到神秘的耦合常数，但那些玩弄算术的人往往没有充分意识到，用 π 和 e 等等可以组成多少个数字，这着实令人惊讶。因此，在现代物理学的历史上，人们发表了一篇又一篇论文，证明电子密度e精确到小数点后几位，但下一轮改进的实验却否定了这一结果。

A good theory would say that e is the square root of 3 over 2 pi squared, or something. There have been, from time to time, suggestions as to what e is, but none of them has been useful. First, Arthur Eddington proved by pure logic that the number the physicists like had to be exactly 136, the experimental number at that time. Then, as more accurate experiments showed the number to be closer to 137, Eddington discovered a slight error in his earlier argument, and showed by pure logic again that the number had to be the integer 137! Every once in a while, someone notices that a certain combination of pi’s and e’s (the base of the natural logarithms), and 2’s and 5’s produces the mysterious coupling constant, but it is a fact not fully appreciated by people who play with arithmetic that you would be surprised how many numbers you can make out of pi’s and e’s and so on. Therefore, throughout the history of modern physics, there has been paper after paper by people who have produced an e to several decimal places, only to have the next round of improved experiments disagree with it.

尽管我们现在只能用一些笨办法来计算j，但或许有一天我们会发现j和e之间存在某种合理的数学联系。这意味着 j 就是那个神秘的数字，而e 正是由此衍生而来。如果真是如此，无疑会涌现出另一批论文，告诉我们如何“徒手”计算j ，比如提出j等于 1 除以 4π 之类的。

Even though we have to resort to a dippy process to calculate j today, it’s possible that someday a legitimate mathematical connection between j and e will be found. That would mean that; is the mysterious number, and from it comes e. In such a case there would doubtless be another batch of papers that tell us how to calculate j “with our bare hands,” so to speak, proposing that j is 1 divided by 4 * pi, or something.

这就暴露了量子电动力学的所有问题。

That exposes all the problems associated with quantum electrodynamics.
